popularStory.description: Eleanor of England (Spanish: Leonor; c.1161 – 31 October 1214), was Queen of Castile and Toledo[3] as wife of Alfonso VIII of Castile. She was the sixth child and second daughter of Henry II, King of …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alfonso De León IX於1171年8月15日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安薩莫拉時，他的父親Ferdinand II De León34歲，母親Urraca Of Portugal23歲。他於1191年與Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal在葡萄牙布拉加吉馬奧斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1230年9月24日在西班牙加利西亞盧戈薩利亞享年59歲，埋葬在西班牙加利西亞A 可盧納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alfonso De León IX于1171年8月15日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安薩莫拉时，他的父亲Ferdinand II De León34岁，母亲Urraca Of Portugal23岁。他于1191年与Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal在葡萄牙布拉加吉馬奧斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1230年9月24日在西班牙加利西亞盧戈薩利亞享年59岁，埋葬在西班牙加利西亞A 可盧納。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably a compound of adal ‘noble’ + funs ‘ready’; a traditional royal name.

variants.name: Alonso
meanings: Variant of   Alfonso .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Alfonso De León IX was born on 15 August 1171, in Zamora, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Ferdinand II De León, was 34 and his mother, Urraca Of Portugal, was 23. He married Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal in 1191, in Guimarães, Braga, Portugal. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 24 September 1230, in Sarria, Lugo, Galicia, Spain, at the age of 59, and was buried in A Coruña, Galicia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alfonso De León IX est né le 15 août 1171, à Zamora, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Ferdinand II De León, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Urraca Of Portugal, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal en 1191, à Guimarães, Braga, Portugal. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1230, à Sarria, Lugo, Galicia, Espagne, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à A Coruña, Galicia, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alfonso De León IX am 15. August 1171 in Zamora, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ferdinand II De León 34 und seine Mutter Urraca Of Portugal 23. Er hat 1191 in Guimarães, Braga, Portugal Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 24. September 1230 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Sarria, Lugo, Galicia, Spanien und wurde in A Coruña, Galicia, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alfonso De León IX è nato il il 15 agosto 1171 nel Zamora, Castilla y León, Spagna, il suo padre, Ferdinand II De León, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Urraca Of Portugal, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal nel 1191, nel Guimarães, Braga, Portogallo. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 24 settembre 1230, nel Sarria, Lugo, Galicia, Spagna, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a A Coruña, Galicia, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Alfonso De León IXが1171年8月15日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンザモーアで生まれた時，父Ferdinand II De Leónは34歳，母Urraca Of Portugalは23歳であった。1191年にポルトガルブラガグイマルaスでBlessed Teresa Sanches of Portugalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1230年9月24日にスペインガリシアルゴサリアで59歳で死去し，スペインガリシアア コルnアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alfonso De León IX(이)가 1171년 8월 15일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 자모라에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ferdinand II De León(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Urraca Of Portugal(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1191년 포르투갈 브라가 길마a스에서 Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1230년 9월 24일 스페인 갈리샤 루고 사리아에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 갈리샤 에이 코루n아에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alfonso De León IX nasceu em 15 de agosto de 1171, em Zamora, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Ferdinand II De León, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Urraca Of Portugal, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal em 1191, em Guimarães, Braga, Portugal. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de setembro de 1230, em Sarria, Lugo, Galicia, Espanha, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em A Coruña, Galicia, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alfonso De León IX родился 15 августа 1171 г. в местности замора, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Ferdinand II De León было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Urraca Of Portugal, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal в 1191 г. в местности гуимарaесь, брага, Португалия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 24 сентября 1230 г. в местности сарря, луго, галися, Испания в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности а коруnа, галися, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alfonso De León IX nació el 15 de agosto de 1171, en Zamora, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Ferdinand II De León, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Urraca Of Portugal, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Blessed Teresa Sanches of Portugal en 1191, en Guimarães, Braga, Portugal. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 24 de septiembre de 1230, en Sarria, Lugo, Galicia, España, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en La Coruña, Galicia, España.
